title: centaurea × borzae
intro: Centaurea × borzae, also known as Borza's cornflower or Borza's knapweed, is a hybrid plant derived from the Cornflower (Centaurea cyanus) and the meadow knapweed (Centaurea jacea). It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antitumor activities. The health benefits of Centaurea × borzae include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as a natural pain reliever. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
